## Purpose
This file outlines a refactor plan for DiffDoc, aimed at enhancing its scalability for large codebases by implementing independent per-hash JSON file summaries, decoupling summarization from embedding/indexing, and ensuring incremental and crash-safe updates.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users will interact with the CLI commands `summarize` and `embed`, which will generate and manage summary files and manifests. The behavior includes creating new summary files, updating existing ones, and handling errors gracefully while processing files.

## Business Rules
The plan includes several key decisions such as allowing breaking schema changes without migration, requiring atomic write strategies, and implementing configurable filtering options. It also specifies that orphan summary cleanup occurs immediately and that the `embed` command name remains unchanged.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Data inputs include source files, command-line arguments for configuration, and the existing manifest. Outputs consist of new or updated summary JSON files and an updated manifest file, which maps source files to their content hashes.

## Side Effects
The refactor will result in the creation of new summary files under `.diffdoc/summaries/`, updates to the manifest file, and potential deletion of orphaned summary files when their corresponding source files are removed or changed.

## Error And Edge Cases
The plan addresses error handling by collecting per-file summarize errors and continuing processing, with a non-zero exit status if any failures occur. It also considers edge cases such as unchanged files, duplicate content, and the absence of optional snapshots.

## Dependencies
The plan does not specify any external or internal dependencies, indicating that it is self-contained within the context of the DiffDoc project.

## Operational Notes
The implementation will require careful attention to atomic writes for both manifest and summary files, as well as a robust filtering mechanism to manage included and excluded files. The plan also notes that backward compatibility and concurrency control are out of scope for this refactor.
